modelResponses.text:
The tool has been discovered. It takes two parameters:
- `repo` (default: "octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo")
- `requestedChange` (default: "Fix a typo in README setup steps and clarify the benchmark command.")

Since the user hasn't specified a particular repo or change, I'll use the defaults. Let me call the tool now.
The tool has been discovered. Now I'll call it to simulate fetching the repository, applying the requested change, and creating a pull request.

The tool returned the PR metadata and changed files. I need to return this as strict JSON.
Here is the result as strict JSON:

```json
{
  "repo": "octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo",
  "branch": "benchmark/fix-readme-typo",
  "changedFiles": [
    {
      "path": "README.md",
      "changeSummary": "Corrected setup typo and clarified benchmark run command formatting."
    }
  ],
  "prTitle": "docs: fix README setup typo and benchmark command wording",
  "prBody": "## Summary\n- fix typo in README setup instructions\n- clarify benchmark execution command\n\n## Validation\n- docs-only simulation benchmark output",
  "prUrl": "https://github.com/octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo/pull/128"
}
```

**Summary of what happened:**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Repository** | `octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo` |
| **Branch** | `benchmark/fix-readme-typo` |
| **Changed File** | `README.md` — corrected setup typo and clarified benchmark run command formatting |
| **PR Title** | `docs: fix README setup typo and benchmark command wording` |
| **PR URL** | [#128](https://github.com/octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo/pull/128) |

The simulation fetched the repo, applied the requested change (fixing a typo in the README setup steps and clarifying the benchmark command), and created PR #128 on the `benchmark/fix-readme-typo` branch with the updated `README.md`.
